questions.question:
Match the following :

\( \begin{array}{ll}\text{(i)  2NF}  &\text{(a)  Transitive dependencies eliminated}  \\ \text{(ii)  3NF}  &\text{(b)  Multivalued attribute removed} \\\text{(iii)  3NF}  &\text{(c)  Contains no partial functional dependencies} \\\text{(iv)  5NF}  &\text{(d)  Contains no join dependency}  \end{array} \)



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2004 | Part 2 | Question: 20

Suppose R is a relation schema and F is a set of functional dependencies on R. Further, suppose R1 and R2 form a decomposition of R. Then the decomposition is a lossless join decomposition of R provided that:



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2008 | Part 2 | Question: 32
options: both R1∩R2→R1 and R1∩R2→R2 functional dependencies are in F+
at least one from R1∩R2→R1 and R1∩R2→R2 is in F+
questions.correct_answer: at least one from R1∩R2→R1 and R1∩R2→R2 is in F+

questions.question:
Match the following :

\( \begin{array}{clcl}  \text{(1)} & \text{Determinants}  & \text{(a)}\ & \text{No attribute can be added} \\   \text{(2)} & \text{Candidate key}  & \text{(b)}\ & \text{Uniquely identified a row} \\  \text{(3)} & \text{Non-redundancy}  & \text{(c)}\ & \text{A constraint between two attribute} \\   \text{(4)} & \text{Functional Dependency}  & \text{(d)}\ & \text{Group of attributes on the left hand} \\ &&&  \text{side of arrow of function dependency.} \\  \end{array} \)



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2009 | Part 2 | Question: 18

A function that has no partial functional dependencies is in __________ form.
(A) 3 NF

(B) 2 NF

(C) 4 NF

(D) BCNF


Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2009 | Part 2 | Question: 19

The dependency preservation decomposition is a property to decompose database schema D, in which each functional dependency \( X \rightarrow Y \) specified in \( F \),



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2010 | Part 2 | Question: 17
options: Appeared directly in one of the relation schemas \( Ri \) in the decomposed D.
Could be inferred from dependencies that appear in some \( Ri \).

questions.question:
Which of the following is true?



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 2 | Question: 13
options: A relation in BCNF is always in 3NF
A relation in 3NF is always in BCNF
A relation in BCNF is not in 3NF
questions.correct_answer: A relation in BCNF is always in 3NF

questions.question:
The third normal form is based on the concept of_____



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 63

Armstrong \( (1974) \) proposed systematic approach to derive functional dependencies. Match the following w.r.t functional dependencies:

\( \begin{array}{}  & \textbf{List-I}  && \textbf{List -II} \\  \text{a.} &  \text{Decomposition Rule} & \text{i.} & \text{If \)X \rightarrow Y\( and \)Z \rightarrow W\( then \)\{X,Z\} \rightarrow \{Y, W\}\( } \\  \text{b.} &  \text{Union rule} & \text{ii.} & \text{If \)X \rightarrow Y\( and \)\{Y, W\} \rightarrow Z\( then \)\{X, W\} \rightarrow Z\( } \\  \text{c.} &  \text{Composition rule} & \text{iii.} & \text{If \)X \rightarrow Y\( and \)X \rightarrow Z\( then \)X \rightarrow \{Y, Z\}\( } \\  \text{d.} &  \text{Psedudo transitivity rule} & \text{iv.} & \text{ If \)X \rightarrow \{Y, Z\}\( then \)X \rightarrow Y\( and \)X \rightarrow Z\( } \\  \end{array} \)

Codes:



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 59

The best normal form of relation scheme \( R(A, B, C, D) \) along with the set of functional dependencies \( F = \left\{AB \rightarrow C, AB \rightarrow D, C \rightarrow A, D \rightarrow B\right\} \) is



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2014 | Part 2 | Question: 19

Identify the minimal key for relational scheme \( R(A, B, C, D, E) \) with functional dependencies \( F = \left\{A \rightarrow B, B \rightarrow C, AC \rightarrow D\right\} \) 



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2014 | Part 2 | Question: 20

Let \( R = ABCDE \) is a relational scheme with functional dependency set \( F = \left\{A \rightarrow B, B \rightarrow C, AC \rightarrow D\right\} \). The attribute closures of \( A \) and \( E \) are 



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 10

Which of the following is false ? 



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 12
options: Every binary relation is never be in \( BCNF \).
\( 1 NF, 2 NF, 3 NF \) and \( BCNF \) are based on functional dependencies.
Multivalued Dependency \( (MVD) \) is a special case of Join Dependency \( (JD) \)
questions.correct_answer: Every binary relation is never be in \( BCNF \).

questions.question:
A relation R={A,B,C,D,E,F,G} is given with following set of functional dependencies: F={AD→E, BE→F, B→C, AF→G}. Which of the following is a candidate key?



Databases: UGC NET CSE | December 2015 | Part 2 | Question: 20

For database relation \( R(A,B,C,D) \) where the domains of \( A,B,C \) and \( D \) include only atomic values, only the following functional dependencies and those that can be inferred from them are:

\( A \rightarrow C \)

\( B \rightarrow D \)

The relation \( R \) is in



Databases: UGC NET CSE | January 2017 | Part 3 | Question: 10
options: First normal form but not in second normal form
Both in first normal form as well as in second normal form
Second normal form but not in third normal form.
Both in second normal form as well as in third normal form.
questions.correct_answer: First normal form but not in second normal form

questions.question:
Multi-valued dependency among attribute is checked at which level ?



Databases: UGC NET CSE | June 2005 | Part 2 | Question: 19

Match the following :

\( \begin{array}{clccl}   \text{a.} & \text{2 NF} & & \text{i.} & \text{Transitive dependencies eliminated} \\  \text{b.} & \text{3 NF} & & \text{ii.} & \text{Multivalued attribute removed} \\ \text{c.} & \text{4 NF} & & \text{iii.} & \text{Contain no partial Functional dependencies} \\ \text{d.} & \text{5 NF} &  & \text{iv.} & \text{Contains no join dependencies}  \\    \end{array} \)

\( \textbf{Codes :} \)



Databases: UGC NET CSE | June 2010 | Part 2 | Question: 18

For a database relation R(a, b, c, d) where the domains of a, b, c, d include only the atomic values. The functional dependency a \( \rightarrow \) c, b \( \rightarrow \) d holds in the following relation.



Databases: UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 9

Let \( R =\{A, B, C, D, E, F\} \) be a relation schema with the following dependencies \( C \rightarrow F \), \( E \rightarrow A \), \( EC \rightarrow D \), \( A \rightarrow B \). Which of the following is a key for \( R \) ? 



Databases: UGC NET CSE | June 2014 | Part 2 | Question: 49

Consider the following relational schemas for a library database :

Book (Title, Author, Catalog_no, Publisher, Year, Price) Collection(Title, Author, Catalog_no) with the following functional dependencies :

I. Title, Author \( \rightarrow \) Catalog_no

II. Catalog_no \( \rightarrow \) Title, Author, Publisher, Year

III. Publisher, Title, Year \( \rightarrow \) Price Assume (Author, Title) is the key for both schemas. Which one of the following is true ?



Databases: UGC NET CSE | June 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 22
options: Both Book and Collection are in BCNF.

questions.question:
In relational databases, if relation R is in BCNF, then which of the following is true about relation R?



Databases: UGC NET CSE | June 2019 | Part 2 | Question: 34

The Relation

Vendor Order (V_no, V_ord_no, V_name, Qty_sup, unit_price) is in 2NF because



Databases: UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 10
options: Non_key attribute V_name is dependent on V_no which is part of composite key
Non_key attribute V_name is dependent on Qty_sup
Key attribute Qty_sup is dependent on primary key unit_price
Key attribute V_ord_no is dependent on primary key unit_price
questions.correct_answer: Non_key attribute V_name is dependent on V_no which is part of composite key

questions.question:
If every non-key attribute is functionally dependent on the primary key, then the relation is in ________



Databases: UGC NET CSE | November 2017 | Part 3 | Question: 9

Match List I with List II :

\( \begin{array}{ll} 
\text{List I} & \text{List II} \\ \\
\text {(A) BCNF } & \text { (I) It removes multivalued dependency } \\
\text {(B) 3} \mathrm{NF}  & \text { (II) It is not always dependency preserving } \\
\text {(C) 2} \mathrm{NF}  & \text{ (III) It removes transitive dependency} \\
\text {(D) 4} \mathrm{NF} & \text { (IV) It removes partial functional dependency }\end{array} \)
